title: Lack in support in DNAT to `::1`.
body:
### Issue Description

Podman port exposing doesn't expose ports on IPv6 through DNAT while using `-p <destPort>:<containerPort>`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:
1. Set network_backend to "cni" in `/etc/containers/containers.conf`.
2. Set `cni_plugin_dirs`:
```
cni_plugin_dirs = [
  "/usr/local/libexec/cni",
  "/usr/libexec/cni",
  "/usr/local/lib/cni",
  "/usr/lib/cni",
  "/opt/cni/bin",
]
```
4. Set `enable_ipv6` to `true`.
5. Run `podman run -it -p 8080:80 linuxserver/nginx`
6. In a second terminal run `nmap -6 ::1 -p 8080`.
7. Try again with `podman run -it -p 8080:80 -p [::]:8080:80 linuxserver/nginx`.
8. Run `nmap -6 ::1 -p 8080` again.

### Describe the results you received

Results shows that IPv6 port isn't exposed while **not** using `-p [::]:8080:80`.

### Describe the results you expected

I've expected to see exposed port `8080` on IPv6 network by **only** using `-p 8080:80`.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

-

comments.body:
Note that `::1` port forwarding via firewall does not work, there is https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14491 to track support via an extra userspace proxy like we do for rootless.

As for this issue the problem is that podman does not bind the ipv6 address by default even when ipv6 is enabled on the network.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@Luap99 Should this be fixed in Podman?
